markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Document common failures, diagnostics, recovery steps, repair queues, stale state, and missing Postgres behavior. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualFailureRecoveryPages leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualFailureRecoveryPages is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: UserManual failure recovery pages refreshed for non-page freshness drift; targeted and full UserManual proof suites pass against real PostgreSQL; adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: Seeded UserManual recovery wording now documents freshness resync for changed pages, tool entries, feature entries, and legacy aliases; stale 'only changed pages' wording removed from the seeded recovery surface.
Targeted freshness-doc regression plus full UserManual content/API/storage suites passed against real PostgreSQL; cargo check passed with preexisting warnings only.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.
Hubble adversarial review returned PASS; no blocking findings, with one non-blocking future hardening idea to count read receipts on the missing-link path.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-198 requires the UserManual to document common failure modes, diagnostics, recovery steps, repair queues, stale state, and missing-Postgres behavior as a built-in no-context product surface. The code seeds three real PostgreSQL-backed manual pages with accurate, route-anchored content, and a test proves the documented error vocabulary matches live runtime behavior against a real managed PostgreSQL cluster.
findings: seed.rs:1014-1119 defines page_failure_modes_and_recovery, page_repair_queues_and_staleness, and page_missing_postgres_behavior with concrete accurate content: typed error-envelope vocabulary per surface (documents/ingestion/code-nav/retrieval/memory/loom/crdt/usermanual), the four failure families, a recovery map keyed to real repair routes, repair-queue listing routes, and a correct fail-closed missing-Postgres section (no SQLite/mock fallback, managed cluster 127.0.0.1:5544, never delete managed_pgdata).
seed.rs:182-184 registers all three failure-recovery pages into the seed corpus, so they are seeded into PostgreSQL and not orphaned functions.

Real durable authority: migrations/0310_user_manual.sql creates user_manual_pages tables; tests/user_manual_support.rs connects a real PostgresDatabase + PgPool to an isolated schema with the full migration chain and serves the actual Axum routers; the skip_if_no_pg! macro PANICS rather than skipping when PG is absent (spec-realism compliant).

content_tests.rs:164 mt198_documented_failure_modes_match_runtime loads the seeded failure page from PostgreSQL, then drives live endpoints (code-nav 400 bad_request, cloud_model save 403 forbidden) and asserts the manual's documented error codes match what the runtime actually returns - documentation proven against the real native runtime, not a self-authored mock.

Prior v1 freshness-wording concern is fixed and regression-guarded: content_tests.rs:662-669 asserts the recovery guidance says resync covers 'changed pages, tool entries, feature entries, and legacy aliases' and forbids the stale 'only changed pages are written' wording; seed.rs:1000-1007 carries the corrected text.

Green evidence logs exist and show real runs: green-user-manual-content-after-review-fix.log (12 passed incl. mt198), green-user-manual-storage-after-review-fix.log (11 passed incl. migration_0310_creates_user_manual_tables), green-mt204-freshness-docs-verdicts.log (1 passed). No deferred-live escapes or placeholders in seed.rs (grep for todo!/unimplemented!/pending MT-/stub returned zero).

Spec alignment confirmed: master-spec-v02.194 module 10 sections 10.15 and 10.15.8 mandate the no-context UserManual surface covering purpose/workflows/startup/commands/IO/navigation/safety/failure-modes/recovery; MT-198 implements exactly the failure-modes/recovery/repair-queue/missing-postgres slice. Spec anchors used in the pages (10.15.8, 2.3.13.11, 12.7) resolve in the current bundle.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): lifecycle.handoff fields and wp_validator_session are null and the adversarial review is a self-run subagent; per ground rules this is not a failing reason and the proof is independently real.
evidence_checked: D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake Worktrees/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-009-recovery/ (green logs: content, storage, mt204-freshness)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
